prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap, energies in eV.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier "standard"), exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF) in PySCF (version not reported).
Geometry provenance: User-supplied benzene.xyz (12 atoms) from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz; no geometry optimization was performed – this is a single-point calculation (notation: DFT//input xyz).
Solvent / environment: Gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge and multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
SCF convergence: Converged in 8 cycles (scf_converged: true).
Warnings: no warnings reported.
Live log: chemkit streamed the SCF/analysis output to a frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; this path was displayed at run start and can be monitored with tail ‑f.
Integrity: trustworthy = true (all integrity checks passed).

Headline results (gas phase):
  HOMO energy = −7.0468329 eV
  LUMO energy = −0.41574728 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV

These values were obtained with the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill using the settings detailed above (command recorded in cli_invocation for full reproducibility).
